skill.name: delight
skill.description: Helps add tasteful micro-interactions, personality, copy, and celebration moments to user interfaces without harming usability.
skill.summary: Design tasteful delight moments that make interfaces feel polished and memorable.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators, but review found only Markdown examples and design guidance in SKILL.md. No executable shell code, cryptographic implementation, system probing, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Fenced Examples
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown code fences, CSS snippets, and quoted UI copy examples. They do not execute Ruby, shell commands, or any external process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are fenced Markdown examples or literal product copy. There is no interpreter directive, command runner, subprocess call, or data flow from user input.

low_findings.title: Static Keyword Matches Are Descriptive Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits are false positives from normal prose, headings, and interface guidance. No cryptographic API, hash function, scan command, or host inspection behavior appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is UX guidance about delight, sound design, validation, and loading copy. No evidence of cryptography, system reconnaissance, or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Functional interfaces can feel flat, forgettable, or tense at important moments. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code add appropriate delight through motion, copy, sound, and polish.
actual_capabilities: Identifies success, empty, loading, error, and achievement moments that can benefit from delight.
Suggests micro-interactions, hover states, animations, and feedback patterns for frontend interfaces.
Guides product-specific loading messages, error copy, tooltips, and empty state writing.
Recommends when sound, illustration, seasonal touches, or hidden details are appropriate.
Checks that delight remains fast, accessible, skippable, and aligned with the product context.
limitations: Does not provide a complete design system or brand strategy by itself.
Requires product context to choose the right tone and level of playfulness.
Does not include executable assets, image files, or animation libraries.
May be inappropriate for highly regulated flows unless restrained and reviewed.

use_cases.title: Polish a SaaS Workflow
use_cases.description: Add subtle success feedback, empty states, and helpful loading copy to a work-focused dashboard.

use_cases.title: Improve Frontend Interaction Quality
use_cases.description: Choose animation and interaction patterns that make buttons, forms, toggles, and drag actions feel responsive.

use_cases.title: Create Brand-Appropriate Personality
use_cases.description: Decide where playful, elegant, or restrained moments support the product without distracting users.

prompt_templates.title: Find Delight Opportunities
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delight skill to review the onboarding empty state. Suggest three appropriate delight moments that improve clarity and confidence.

prompt_templates.title: Polish Success and Error States
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delight skill on the save flow. Improve the success state, retry state, and error copy while keeping the tone professional.

prompt_templates.title: Design Micro-Interactions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delight skill to propose micro-interactions for buttons, form validation, and drag actions in this React interface.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Delight Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delight skill to create a product-wide delight strategy for a B2B analytics app. Include moments, tone, accessibility, and performance limits.

output_examples.input: Review a blank dashboard state for a new analytics user.
output: Recommend a warm empty state that explains the first import step.
Add a small progress cue after the first data source connects.
Avoid jokes because the workflow is business critical.

output_examples.input: Improve a file upload success moment.
output: Use a quick checkmark animation after upload completion.
Show concise confirmation copy with the uploaded file name.
Reserve confetti for milestone uploads, not every upload.

output_examples.input: Make a loading state more engaging without slowing the task.
output: Use product-specific loading messages tied to the current operation.
Keep animation subtle and support reduced motion settings.
Show real progress when progress information is available.

best_practices: Match each delight moment to user intent, brand tone, and emotional context.
Keep motion fast, accessible, and respectful of reduced motion preferences.
Use delight for meaningful moments such as completion, discovery, and recovery.
anti_patterns: Do not delay core tasks so users can watch an animation.
Do not use playful copy during serious, legal, financial, or destructive actions.
Do not add generic loading jokes that ignore what the product is doing.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create tasteful interface delight through motion, copy, visual personality, sound cues, and celebration moments.

faq.question: Can it be used with professional products?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes restraint and context, so professional products can use subtle feedback and warm copy.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and guidance. Implementation still depends on the application framework and design system.

faq.question: Does it support accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It explicitly warns against blocking flows and recommends accessible, skippable, and reduced-motion-friendly delight.

faq.question: When should I avoid using it?
faq.answer: Avoid heavy delight in urgent, regulated, destructive, or sensitive workflows where clarity matters most.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
